title: Are women less sensitive to anesthesia than men?
summary: Some, but not all, research suggests that more women recall events that happened under anesthesia than men do, and that women wake up from anesthesia faster than men.

title: The Surprising Habit That Could Raise Your Cholesterol
summary: Registered dietitians and doctors explain why not getting enough quality sleep could be raising your cholesterol levels and negatively affecting your metabolic health.

title: World Immunisation Week 2025 - How Maternal Vaccination Protects The Newborn Too
summary: A mother gives her baby immunity even before birth. Vaccines during pregnancy protect both, boosting the baby's defense against infections during the fragile first months of life.

title: The Impact Of Gut Health On A Child’s Overall Well-Being During Summer
summary: Keeping a child’s gut healthy is an important part of protecting their overall health, especially in summer. Hot weather, changes in eating habits, and a higher chance of infections can make the digestive system work harder.

title: Ayurveda offers cure for dangerous infections like Sepsis: Patanjali research
summary: Patanjali Research Institute has found that Ayurvedic phytoconstituents may help control sepsis and prevent acute kidney injury. Published in Biomedicine and Pharmacotherapy, the study highlights plant-based compounds like curcumin and quercetin as potential treatments with minimal side effects.

title: New research links Western diet to inflammatory diseases
summary: Findings indicate that just 2 weeks of adopting Western diet can lead to increased inflammation

title: Scientists Identify Ideal Amount of Boinking for Maximum Mental Health
summary: Scientists have figured out, using statistics, exactly how often you should have sex to safeguard against depression.

title: NIMHANS study identifies DNA methylation markers for early detection of postpartum depressive symptoms
summary: NIMHANS study identifies DNA methylation markers predicting postpartum depression risk in early pregnancy, aiding early intervention.

title: Zamboanga City dengue cases up by 42%
summary: DENGUE cases in Zamboanga City have surged by 42 percent in the first four months of 2025, the City Health Office (CHO) said.

title: 'What I learned from 59-year mental health career'
summary: Rod Morris shares his memories of nearly six decades working in mental health care.
